Patch Set 1:

This code is just a copy from ll_releasepage() which executes under similar circumstances. If it's wrong, then the code in ll_releasepage() is wrong too, isn't it?

Patch Set 1:

Also, the customer reports an assertion failure in cl_env_attach().

However, this function had been removed before LU-8509 was landed:

root@panda:/mnt/seagate/xyratex/lustre-release# git describe --contains
2.8.59^0
root@panda:/mnt/seagate/xyratex/lustre-release# git log|egrep "LU-8509|453327"
    LU-8509 llite: drop_caches hangs in cl_inode_fini()
commit 45332712783a4756bf5930d6bd5f697bbc27acdb

So they are running some broken version of Lustre.

Patch Set 1:

> This code is just a copy from ll_releasepage() which executes under
 > similar circumstances. If it's wrong, then the code in
 > ll_releasepage() is wrong too, isn't it?

No. They are called in different cases. ll_releasepage() is called by page reclaiming routine; but ll_invalidatepage() is triggered by page truncate.

Patch Set 1:

> ll_releasepage() is called by page reclaiming routine; but ll_invalidatepage() is triggered by page truncate.

They do the same, just ll_releasepage() may refuse to release.

Does it make difference in this particular case that they are called inside page reclaim and truncate respectfull?

Patch Set 1:

If so, how?

The issue looks like broken LU-8509 backport to some code base that does not have commit 453327.

Patch Set 1:

Before 453327, ll_releasepage() called cl_env_reenter().

However, LU-8509 was created after 453327, whn ll_releasepage() did not have this call.

If one takes LU-8509 and applies it to pre-453327, the patch will miss cl_env_reenter().

Patch Set 1:

> If so, how?
 > 
 > The issue looks like broken LU-8509 backport to some code base that
 > does not have commit 453327.

Yes, this issue appeared in a FE version that it doesn't have that commit. And this patch is not necessary to to be committed to current master and 2.9. I just had a problem to push this patch to the correponding branch.

Patch Set 1:

I see, thanks.

Patch Set 1:

Sorry about the noise. :)

Patch Set 1:

> Sorry about the noise. :)

no worry, always good to question.

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Needs a more detailed commit message and has style issues, but otherwise looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

Comments above are based on the assumption that this will get pushed to a 2.8 stable branch.

commit.subject: LU-8936 llite: use percpu env correctly in ll_invalidatepage
commit.message:
LU-8936 llite: use percpu env correctly in ll_invalidatepage

This issue is introduced by LU-8509.

In LU-4257, a patch(commit 4533271) is landed to get rid of cl_env hash
table on the client side. From now on, cl_env won't be attached to
the running processes. This made it possible to use cl_env_percpu_get()
in ll_invalidatepage() as in LU-8509.

However, in LLNL FE branch it includes LU-8509 but doesn't have the
corresponding fix of commit 4533271, which caused the problem on this
ticket because an env is already attached to the process when it
calls ll_invalidatepage().

Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ie2a2d8276abf929d90eb3a64d72c0d9c911bf2f2
